lessons.title: One to Five
lessons.topic: Counting to 5 and sounds of K and L

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: If you have one apple and get one more, how many do you have?
questions.hint: Two is a small number, right after one.

questions.question_text: What is the first sound in 'lion'?
questions.hint: Think of a roaring animal in the jungle. It starts with 'L'.

questions.question_text: What’s the sound a dog makes?
questions.hint: It's a friendly, barking noise dogs make.

steps.title: The Kite and the Lions
steps.text: Look! One bright kite flies. Two lions play under it. It is one kite. It is two lions. They are happy!

steps.explanation: We use 'It is' to say what something is.

steps.audio_text: Look over there! One kite is flying high in the sky. Two lions are watching it go up and down.

questions.question: How many lions are watching the kite?

questions.question_text: What comes after two? (Vocabulary question)
It is a ___. (Fill in with the right word: lion or kite)
True or False: A kite can fly.
How many items are there? 🦁🦁🪁 (Reading comprehension question)

lessons.title: Six to Ten
lessons.topic: Counting to 10 and the sound of M

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: How do the lions move in the video?
questions.hint: Think about how fast lions can be.

questions.question_text: What sound does a lion make?
questions.hint: It's a loud sound like a king!

questions.question_text: How many times do you hear the lion roar in the video?

items.example: How many mouths do you have?

steps.title: Monkeys and Mouths
steps.text: It is morning in the jungle. I see six monkeys swinging in the trees. Look! It is a mouth of a big monkey. There are ten bananas on the ground. How many monkeys? Let's count them.

steps.explanation: Use 'I see' to say what you look at.

steps.audio_text: Listen closely! There are six monkeys swinging and playing. One monkey has a smile on its mouth!

questions.question: What is one monkey doing with its mouth?

questions.question_text: What is the number after six?
I see a ___. (Use a lesson word)
What sound does 'mouth' start with?
True or False: Six is more than ten.
Which word rhymes with 'hen'?

lessons.title: How Many?
lessons.topic: Asking about quantity and sounds of N and O

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: Which animal starts with the letter 'O' and lives in the sea?
questions.hint: This animal has eight arms and loves the ocean.

questions.question_text: How does the octopus move in the water?
questions.hint: Think about how fish move in the water.

questions.question_text: N is the sound of a letter. What sound does 'N' make in 'nose'?
questions.hint: Listen to the first sound in 'nose'.

items.example: How many arms? It is eight.

steps.title: Animal Adventure
steps.text: Look at the sea. It is one octopus. How many monkeys? It is nine. My nose smells the sea. I see a big orange fish!

steps.explanation: Use 'How many' to ask about numbers.

items.sentence: How many apple do you see?
How many book on the table?

steps.audio_text: I have nine noses drawn on my paper and an octopus with orange spots is next to them. Do you like octopuses?

questions.question: How many noses are drawn on the paper?
What spots does the octopus have?

questions.question_text: How many legs does an octopus have?
The letter 'N' sounds like ___ in 'nine'.
True or False: An orange is a fruit.
What word starts with 'N' and is on your face?

steps.instruction: Watch the video and answer these questions!

questions.question_text: What sound does the letter 'O' make?
Which number comes after 3?
Which fruit is red and starts with 'A'?
questions.hint: This fruit is crunchy and starts with 'A'.

steps.title: Sam's Playground
steps.text: Hi! I am Sam. I see two slides and five swings. It is fun! I have a red ball. How many swings? Five!

questions.question: How many swings does Sam see?

steps.audio_text: I have four blue blocks and three green cars. My sister asked, 'How many cars do we have?' I said, 'We have three!'

questions.question: How many cars do we have?

steps.prompt: Say: How many cats do you see?

questions.question_text: It ___ four. (Grammar question)
Which number is in between 6 and 8? (Reading comprehension question)
How many oranges are there if you see three? (Grammar question)
True or False: A lemon is yellow.
Which word begins with 'K'? (Vocabulary question)
